## Purpose
This file serves as the configuration for the Node.js package "diffdoc," which translates repository code shifts into plain-English business context.

## User-Visible Behavior
Users can install the package and utilize the CLI commands `diffdoc` and `diffdoc-mcp` to execute the functionality provided by the package. The package can be built and started using defined scripts.

## Business Rules
The package requires Node.js version 22 or higher. It includes scripts for building the project, cleaning the output directory, and starting the application.

## Data Inputs And Outputs
The package accepts inputs through command-line arguments when executing the CLI commands. It outputs results based on the translation of code shifts into business context.

## Side Effects
The `clean` script deletes the `dist` directory, and the `build` script generates compiled output in the `dist` directory.

## Error And Edge Cases
None identified.

## Dependencies
The package relies on several external libraries, including @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, commander, ignore, openai, simple-git, vectra, and zod for its functionality. It also includes development dependencies like @types/node and typescript.

## Operational Notes
The package includes a build process using TypeScript and requires the `dist` directory to be present for execution. The `prepare` script ensures the package is built before publishing.
